page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n1 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The processor includes an L3 cache of 45 MB, an L2 cache of 0.25 MB, and an L1d cache of 32 KB. The instance is configured with 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the n1-standard-4 delivers modest computational and memory throughput. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks register in the bottom 10%. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score reaches the middle 50% (average), uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths remain in the bottom 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET commands and PassMark database tests, also place in the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-standard-4 offers an entry-level general-purpose resource profile based on older-generation Intel Xeon hardware. The primary tradeoff is its lower performance-per-resource ratio compared to newer instance families, alongside the complete absence of local NVMe storage. Given these performance characteristics, this instance is best suited for low-intensity general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight background tasks that do not require high-speed local storage or intensive parallel processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 15.0 GB of RAM. It contains 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, a 45 MB L3 cache, and lacks bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests scoring in the bottom 25%, and single-threaded compression tasks falling into the bottom 10%. The PassMark memory score represents a moderate exception, landing in the middle 50%. Qualitatively, the n1-standard-4 represents an entry-level general-purpose tier with a modest performance-to-resource profile. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, development environments, and basic web serving where high-throughput computing and local storage are not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering entry-level performance for basic workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory allocation for lightweight virtualized workloads.

Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture running at 2.3 GHz
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
